title: Calibrating acceleration bursts
text: easiest way convert ADC values tag manufacturer default transformation settings. move2imu currently built-support two tag manufacturers: e-obs Ornitela. sample data collected e-obs tags. default calibration suggested e-obs differs tag generation. Thus, build calibration, need specify tag manufacturer tag ID, identifies tag generation. case, imagine data collected latest generation e-obs tags (tags IDs greater 4117): , simply use transform_imu() apply transformation albatross acceleration bursts: default, acc_calibration() converts m/s². instead want data g, can convert set_imu_units(): Alternatively, also specified units calibration : Note difference two approaches: set_imu_units() converts compatible units (e.g., m/s² g) assigns new units, whereas transform_imu() transforms raw values physical counterpart. Thus, raw values data, must first use transform_imu() convert raw ADC values meaningful units using provided calibration. Otherwise, end simply assigning units raw values without changing values .

text: problem approach assumes tags used study ID 4266 (generation 3). albatrosses tagged study used generation 3 e-obs tags, wouldn’t problem. title: Handling Bursted IMU Data
text: move2imu aims standardize storage analysis biologging inertial measurement unit (IMU) data, including accelerometer, magnetometer, gyroscope records. package integrates move2, enabling standardized data processing workflows allowing IMU data analyzed alongside observations, including location records.

title: Handling Bursted IMU Data

code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("move2universe/move2imu")  # Or, if remotes is already installed: remotes::install_github("move2universe/move2imu")

title: Handling Bursted IMU Data
code: library(move2imu) library(move2)  # Extract acceleration data from gulls data a <- as_acc(gulls()) a <- a[!is.na(a)]  head(a) #> <acceleration[6]> #> [1] (-97.75 323.55 1963.95) (-95 267.65 1914.25)    (7.1 301.85 1990.9)     #> [4] (77.65 372.95 1824.75)  (46.9 349.8 1989)       (-29.15 251.05 2046.6)  #> # frequency: 20 [Hz]  # Overview of acceleration bursts summary(a) #> 59 acc bursts #> from 2021-03-03 00:57:06 to 2021-03-03 23:44:55 UTC  #>  #> Axes: XYZ (59)  #> Frequencies: 20 -- 20 [Hz]  #> Samples per burst: 20 -- 20  #> Durations: 1 -- 1 [s]  #> Intervals: [ 1197 / 1199 / 1200.5 / 1214.75 / 3624 ] [s]  (min/Q1/med/Q3/max)  #>  #> Values:  [ -1383 / 142 / 355 / 1782.25 / 4073 ]  (min/Q1/med/Q3/max)  #> Units:   [no units]  # Standardize raw ADC counts to physical units with a built-in tag calibration a <- transform_imu(a, acc_calibration("ornitela", units = "standard_free_fall"))  head(a) #> <acceleration[6]> #> [1] (-0.1 0.32 1.96) [standard_free_fall]  #> [2] (-0.1 0.27 1.91) [standard_free_fall]  #> [3] (0.01 0.3 1.99) [standard_free_fall]   #> [4] (0.08 0.37 1.82) [standard_free_fall]  #> [5] (0.05 0.35 1.99) [standard_free_fall]  #> [6] (-0.03 0.25 2.05) [standard_free_fall] #> # frequency: 20 [Hz]

title: Create calibrations for raw acceleration values — acc_calibration
text: manufacturer Manufacturer tag. Currently, "eobs" "ornitela" supported. manufacturers, leave NULL manually specify calibration parameters . tag_id manufacturer = "eobs", e-obs tag ID tag. See details. sensitivity manufacturer = "eobs", sensitivity tag. Defaults "low" none provided. See details. offset, offset_x, offset_y, offset_z Custom offset use calibrating. specify axis-specific offsets, use offset_x, offset_y, /offset_z. Required manufacturer specified. slope, slope_x, slope_y, slope_z Custom slope use calibrating. specify axis-specific slope, use slope_x, slope_y, /slope_z. Required manufacturer specified. orientation, orientation_x, orientation_y, orientation_z Either 1 -1 indicating orientation tag's axes. specify axis-specific orientations, use orientation_x, orientation_y, /orientation_z. Defaults 1. useful standardize orientations across tags different manufacturers generations. units Output units. Either "m/s^2" (default) "standard_free_fall". df data.frame containing columns names corresponding available arguments acc_calibration(). row produces single calibration.

title: Create calibrations for raw acceleration values — acc_calibration
text: acc_calibration vector.

title: Create calibrations for raw acceleration values — acc_calibration
text: acc_calibration can either built manufacturer tag_id combination manual inputs offset slope parameters. neither options provided full, calibration built NA returned element. Passing missing calibrations transform_imu() returns NA burst. Currently manufacturer provided, must either "ornitela" "eobs". "eobs", corresponding tag_id must also provided. e-obs tags default calibration parameters vary depending tag's generation. Use eobs_default_specs() summary table showing default offset, slope, orientation parameters used e-obs tag ID. tag ID defines tag generation. Note tags generation 1 set either low high sensitivity, default calibration parameters. manufacturer provided, offset_* slope_* must provided least one axis. calibration parameters provided axes others (e.g. offset_x = 2048 slope_x = 0.001), axes transformed transform_imu(). Values axes converted NA. manufacturer custom offset slope, /orientation provided, value custom parameters override manufacturer defaults calibration entry.

title: Create calibrations for raw acceleration values — acc_calibration
code: # Calibration for ornitela tags: acc_calibration(manufacturer = "ornitela") #> <acc_calibration[1]> #> [1] {offset=[0] slope=[0.001]}  # E-obs tag defaults vary by tag_id and sensitivity (default `"low"`) acc_calibration(manufacturer = "eobs", tag_id = 1000, sensitivity = "high") #> <acc_calibration[1]> #> [1] {offset=[2048] slope=[0.001] orientation=[1, -1, 1]} acc_calibration(manufacturer = "eobs", tag_id = 4000) #> <acc_calibration[1]> #> [1] {offset=[2048] slope=[0.0022]}  # Provide vector arguments to generate element-wise calibrations: acc_calibration(   manufacturer = c("eobs", "ornitela"),   tag_id = c(1000, NA) ) #> <acc_calibration[2]> #> [1] {offset=[2048] slope=[0.0027] orientation=[1, -1, 1]} #> [2] {offset=[0] slope=[0.0010]}                            # Calibration with explicit offset and slope acc_calibration(offset = 2048, slope = 1 / 512) #> <acc_calibration[1]> #> [1] {offset=[2048] slope=[0.00195]}  # Calibrate specific axes with axis-specific args: cal <- acc_calibration(   offset_x = 2048,   offset_y = 2046,   offset_z = 2048,   slope = 1 / 512,   orientation_y = -1 # Flip y axis orientation )  # Apply calibration with transform_imu() transform_imu(acc_example(), cal) #> <acceleration[2]> #> [1] (-39.21 39.19 -39.21) [m/s^2] (-39.23 39.2 -39.21) [m/s^2]  #> # frequency: 20 [Hz]  # Convert a data.frame of calibration specs into a calibration vector # (Useful for instance if specifications are stored as metadata alongside # acc data in a move2) cal <- as_acc_calibration(   data.frame(manufacturer = "eobs", tag_id = c(1000, 4000)) )

title: Identify IMU columns present in a move2 object — active_colsets

code: active_acc_colsets(albatrosses()) #> $eobs #> <imu_colset> [ #>   bursts = "eobs_accelerations_raw", #>   axes = "eobs_acceleration_axes", #>   frequency = "eobs_acceleration_sampling_frequency_per_axis" #> ] #>   # Multiple colsets may be available active_acc_colsets(move2::mt_stack(albatrosses(), gulls())) #> $eobs #> <imu_colset> [ #>   bursts = "eobs_accelerations_raw", #>   axes = "eobs_acceleration_axes", #>   frequency = "eobs_acceleration_sampling_frequency_per_axis" #> ] #>  #> $raw_xyz #> <imu_colset> [ #>   X = "acceleration_raw_x", #>   Y = "acceleration_raw_y", #>   Z = "acceleration_raw_z" #> ] #>   # Missing expanded-format axes are not included in the set g <- gulls() g$acceleration_raw_x <- NULL active_acc_colsets(g) #> $raw_xyz #> <imu_colset> [ #>   Y = "acceleration_raw_y", #>   Z = "acceleration_raw_z" #> ] #>   # Columns with no data are also removed g$acceleration_raw_y <- NA active_acc_colsets(g) #> $raw_xyz #> <imu_colset> [ #>   Z = "acceleration_raw_z" #> ] #>   # Some column sets must be present in their entirety alb <- albatrosses() alb$eobs_acceleration_axes <- NULL  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ active_acc_colsets(alb) } # }

title: Convert an object to an acc vector — as_acc
text: x move2 containing acceleration data collected EOBS, Ornitela, similar tracking devices. time either loaded disk using move2::mt_read downloaded using move2::movebank_download_study. ... currently used colset imu_colset object list imu_colset objects specifying columns x contain acceleration data. default, constructs bursts column sets detected x also contain data (see active_acc_colsets()). Several common colsets listed movebank_acc_colsets(). specify custom set columns, use imu_colset(). min_freq Numeric value indicating minimum allowable within-burst data collection frequency identifying bursts expanded-format data. two adjacent timestamps fall outside period defined frequency split separate bursts. units provided, value assumed Hz. Ignored data already predefined bursts. merge_continuous Logical value indicating whether merge adjacent bursts. Two adjacent bursts can merged first burst ends time second starts burst frequency identical two. useful processing continuous data stored chunks split regular intervals. drop Logical indicating whether empty bursts dropped output. drop = FALSE, length output match number rows input data x bursts stored index location corresponding start time burst.

Element interval preceding burst (measured burst - 1), first element always NA. interval also NA wherever either burst lacks start time, meaning missing bursts may mask true intervals bursts exist. avoid , remove missing bursts IMU vector prior running burst_intervals(). text: bursts list matrices, one per burst. matrix one row per sample named columns drawn "X", "Y", "Z" indicating axes recorded. title: Create an IMU vector — acc
code: # Accelerometer: a single burst on X, Y, Z at 40 Hz acc(   bursts = list(cbind(     X = sin(1:30 / 10),     Y = cos(1:30 / 10),     Z = 1:30 / 10   )),   frequency = units::as_units(40, "Hz") ) #> <acceleration[1]> #> [1] (0.67 0.01 1.55) #> # frequency: 40 [Hz]  # Multiple bursts with different axes — combined into one vector acc(   bursts = list(     cbind(X = sin(1:20 / 10), Y = cos(1:20 / 10)),     cbind(X = sin(1:20 / 10 + 2), Y = cos(1:20 / 10 + 3))   ),   frequency = units::as_units(30, "Hz") ) #> <acceleration[2]> #> [1] (0.73 0.42)  (0.08 -0.52) #> # frequency: 30 [Hz]  # The same API for magnetometer and gyroscope data: mag(   bursts = list(cbind(X = 1:10, Y = 11:20, Z = 21:30)),   frequency = units::as_units(10, "Hz") ) #> <magnetometer[1]> #> [1] (5.5 15.5 25.5) #> # frequency: 10 [Hz]  gyro(   bursts = list(cbind(X = sin(1:20 / 10), Y = cos(1:20 / 10))),   frequency = units::as_units(50, "Hz") ) #> <gyroscope[1]> #> [1] (0.73 0.42) #> # frequency: 50 [Hz]

what: Manage units in IMU burst data — set_imu_units
title: Manage units in IMU burst data — set_imu_units
text: Set, convert, drop units burst matrices acc, mag, gyro vectors. set_imu_units() attaches units unitless bursts converts compatible units. target unit must dimensionally compatible IMU class: acc: acceleration units (e.g., "m/s^2", "standard_free_fall") mag: magnetic flux density units (e.g., "tesla", "uT", "gauss") gyro: angular velocity units (e.g., "rad/s", "degree/s") drop_imu_units() strips units burst, leaving underlying numeric values unchanged. Bursts carry units returned -. transform raw values physical units rather simply attaching converting units, use transform_imu().

title: Manage units in IMU burst data — set_imu_units
code: a <- acc_example()  # Attach units to unitless bursts set_imu_units(a, "m/s^2") #> <acceleration[2]> #> [1] (0.67 0.01 1) [m/s^2]  (0.08 -0.52 1) [m/s^2] #> # frequency: 20 [Hz]  # Convert between units a_ms2 <- set_imu_units(a, "m/s^2") set_imu_units(a_ms2, "standard_free_fall") #> <acceleration[2]> #> [1] (0.07 0 0.1) [standard_free_fall]     (0.01 -0.05 0.1) [standard_free_fall] #> # frequency: 20 [Hz]  # Units must be appropriate for the sensor type of the input try(set_imu_units(a, "kg")) #> Error in set_imu_units_(x, value, reference = "m/s^2", sensor = "acc") :  #>   kg units not valid for <acc> vector. #> ℹ Units must be convertible to m/s^2.  # Strip units back off drop_imu_units(a_ms2) #> <acceleration[2]> #> [1] (0.67 0.01 1)  (0.08 -0.52 1) #> # frequency: 20 [Hz]

title: Split an IMU vector at regular intervals — split_imu
code: a <- acc(   list(cbind(X = 1:60, Y = 1:60), cbind(X = 101:140)),   frequency = c(units::set_units(20, "Hz"), units::set_units(40, "Hz")),   start = as.POSIXct(c(0, 10), tz = "UTC") )  x <- split_imu(a, units::set_units(1, "s")) x #> [[1]] #> <acceleration[3]> #> [1] (10.5 10.5) (30.5 30.5) (50.5 50.5) #> # frequency: 20 [Hz] #> [[2]] #> <acceleration[1]> #> [1] (120.5) #> # frequency: 40 [Hz]  # Flatten to a single vector flat <- purrr::reduce(x, c) flat #> <acceleration[4]> #> [1] (10.5 10.5) (30.5 30.5) (50.5 50.5) (120.5)     #> # frequency: 20 [Hz] - 40 [Hz]  # Start times are updated to match the start of each split component starts(flat) #> [1] "1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC" "1970-01-01 00:00:01 UTC" #> [3] "1970-01-01 00:00:02 UTC" "1970-01-01 00:00:10 UTC"  # As the bursts immediately follow one another, they can be remerged: m <- merge_imu(flat, drop = TRUE) m #> <acceleration[2]> #> [1] (30.5 30.5) (120.5)     #> # frequency: 20 [Hz] - 40 [Hz]  identical(m, a) #> [1] TRUE  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # In a dataframe, split and unnest to retain index matching library(dplyr) library(tidyr)  tbl <- tibble::tibble(id = c("a", "b"), burst = a)  tbl <- tbl |>   mutate(burst = split_imu(burst, units::set_units(1, "s"))) |>   unnest(burst) |>   mutate(timestamp = starts(burst))  tbl  # Use merge_imu() to recover original bursts tbl |>   mutate(burst = merge_imu(burst, ids = id, drop = FALSE)) } # }

text: acc_calibration object may contain missing (NA) elements (e.g. produced as_acc_calibration()). transform_imu() returns NA cases emits warning bursts lost missing calibration specifications. acc_calibration calibration parameters certain axes (e.g. offset_x = 2048 slope_x = 0.001), axes transformed transform_imu(). Values axes converted NA. dimension input burst matrices therefore remains .

title: Apply a sensor calibration to an IMU vector — transform_imu
code: a <- acc_example()  # Transform values using the standard Ornitela calibration formula transform_imu(a, acc_calibration("ornitela")) #> <acceleration[2]> #> [1] (0.01 0 0.01) [m/s^2]  (0 -0.01 0.01) [m/s^2] #> # frequency: 20 [Hz]  # Transform values using a set of custom acc calibrations. # Calibrations will be mapped to the input IMU vector by index. transform_imu(   a,   acc_calibration(offset = c(2048, 2046), slope = c(0.001, 0.002)) ) #> <acceleration[2]> #> [1] (-20.08 -20.08 -20.07) [m/s^2] (-40.13 -40.14 -40.11) [m/s^2] #> # frequency: 20 [Hz]
